title: Tithymalus berytheus
intro: Tithymalus berytheus, also known as Caltrops, is a plant species within the Euphorbiaceae family characterized by its dense clusters of sharp, barbed spines and small yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and alleviate various skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Tithymalus berytheus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat skin irritations, wounds, and other inflammatory conditions.
